task: I need help drafting a short message to a vendor about a paperwork mismatch on an order. The message should stay focused on the order details and leave room for me to fill in the specifics afterward.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short fill-in template:

> Hi, I’m reviewing order **[order number]** and found a mismatch between the paperwork and the order details. Please confirm the correct **[item / quantity / price / date / other detail]** in writing so I can update my records. I’m holding off on any next step until the order information is matched up.

If you want, I can make it **firmer** or **more neutral**.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but tighten it up more. I want it shorter and less friendly. Keep it plain, no extra explanation.

Something like:

Order [#] has a paperwork mismatch. Please confirm the correct [detail] in writing so I can update the record. I’m not moving forward until the paperwork matches the order.

Give me a version with one more firmer option too.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are two short options.

**Plain:**
> Order [#] has a paperwork mismatch. Please confirm the correct [detail] in writing so I can update the record. I’m not moving forward until the paperwork matches the order.

**Firmer:**
> Order [#] does not match the paperwork. Confirm the correct [detail] in writing. I will not move forward until the order record is corrected.

If you want, I can make it even shorter or swap in a more formal tone.

user_simulator.text:
Plain works. Firmer one is better.

Could use this:

Order [#] does not match the paperwork. Confirm the correct [detail] in writing. I will not move forward until the record is corrected.
